%%% Helper function to calculate LMax array

prefix_max(Nums) ->

            % State is {CurrentMax, LMaxListReversed}
            {_, LMaxRev} = lists:foldl(fun(CurrentMax, N) ->
                {max(CurrentMax, N), CurrentMax}

%%% Helper function to calculate LMax array
%%% LMax[i] stores the maximum element in nums[0..i-1].
%%% The resulting list has the same length as nums.
%%% Returns a list of integers.
prefix_max(Nums) ->
    case Nums of
        [] -> [];
        [H|T] ->
            % State is {CurrentMax, LMaxListReversed}
            {_, LMaxRev} = lists:foldl(fun(CurrentMax, N) ->
                {max(CurrentMax, N), CurrentMax}
            end, -1, [H|T]),
            lists:reverse(LMaxRev)
    end,
    where
        N = length(Nums).

%%% Helper function to calculate RMax array
%%% RMax[i] stores the maximum element in nums[i+1..N-1].
%%% Achieved by reversing, applying prefix_max, and reversing back.
prefix_max_reversed(Nums) ->
    ReversedNums = lists:reverse(Nums),
    LMaxPrime = prefix_max(ReversedNums),
    lists:reverse(LMaxPrime).

%%% Main solving function
solve(Nums) ->
    case Nums of
        [] -> [];
        _ ->
            LMax = prefix_max(Nums),
            RMax = prefix_max_reversed(Nums),
            
            % Iterate and filter
            ValidElements = lists:filter(fun(Value, Index) ->
                % Check if Value > LMax[Index] OR Value > RMax[Index]
                Value > ListElement(LMax, Index) ;
                Value > ListElement(RMax, Index)
            end, Nums, 0),
            ValidElements.

%%% Helper to retrieve element from list by index (since standard Erlang lists are slow for indexing)
%%% However, since N <= 100, repeated ListElement calls are acceptable for simplicity of implementation.
ListElement([], _) -> error(invalid_index/1);
ListElement([H|T], 0) -> H;
ListElement([_|T], N) -> ListElement(T, N - 1).
```
